topic: Someone discovers their teenager has a secret social media account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The silence in the house was a heavy, suffocating blanket. Liam stared at the chipped paint on the windowsill, the afternoon light doing nothing to lift the dull ache in his chest. He’d spent the last hour trying to tune out the low thrum of his sister’s music from downstairs, anything to avoid the inevitable confrontation. Then, he found it: a forgotten password note tucked inside a textbook. It led to a profile picture that made his stomach clench—a smiling face he didn't recognize, posting curated snapshots of parties and effortless confidence. A wave of hot shame washed over him, making his palms sweat. He felt physically ill, a tight knot forming beneath his ribs. He knew, with a sinking certainty, that this hidden world was a gaping chasm between who his sister presented to the world and who she truly was.

[story 2]

"Just leave me alone," Sarah mumbled, pulling her headphones tighter over her ears, the volume cranked high enough to drown out the incessant, buzzing anxiety in her own head. Every glance from her parents felt like a spotlight, exposing some hidden flaw she couldn't quite mask. She was trying to focus on the textbook, but the words swam before her eyes. Then, her phone buzzed, a notification she hadn't expected. Curiosity, sharp and unwelcome, pulled her toward the screen. The feed was vibrant, full of laughter and perfect lighting. A cold dread seeped into her bones as she realized the account belonged to her sister, a secret life flourishing just beyond the reach of her awareness. She quickly minimized the app, her breathing shallow and quick, desperately wanting the floor to swallow her whole.

[story 3]

Mark traced patterns on the dusty wooden floorboards, the silence amplifying the frantic drumming of his own pulse. He felt perpetually adrift, a disconnected observer in his own home. He’d been avoiding his sister all week, the forced politeness grating on his nerves. It was while rummaging through her discarded backpack that he stumbled upon the login details. The account wasn't just a hobby; it was a meticulously constructed persona, a carefully edited version of her existence. A strange, hollow feeling settled in his gut, a profound sense of being excluded from something vital. He looked at the screen, and the carefully constructed facade shattered, leaving him with a heavy, unproductive stillness.
